subject: Cloud Computing
title: Retry with Exponential Backoff
description: A 60-second lesson on Retry with Exponential Backoff.

hook.text: A request fails. You retry immediately. It fails again. You retry faster. Now you're DDoS-ing your own system.

buildup.text: Exponential backoff spaces retries further apart: 1 second, 2 seconds, 4 seconds, 8 seconds, and so on.

discovery.text: Adding random jitter prevents all clients from retrying at the exact same moment — a thundering herd.

twist.text: Without backoff, a temporary glitch becomes a permanent outage. Retries pile up faster than the server can recover.

climax.text: Every cloud SDK uses exponential backoff by default. It's the simplest resilience pattern and one of the most important.

punchline.text: Retry gently. Give the system time to breathe and recover.

quiz.question: Why is random jitter added to exponential backoff?
options: To prevent all clients from retrying at the same time and overwhelming the server
To make requests arrive in alphabetical order
To encrypt retry attempts for security
